system_design_doc.$comment: Facts extracted from NewUM Data System Design Document — reviewed in ticket #185594.

medallion_architecture.bronze: Raw ingestion; full-fidelity, append-only. Schema valid, no transforms, PII tokenized at write.
medallion_architecture.silver: Cleansed, deduplicated, common data model. Null rates < thresholds, RI enforced, quarantine on failure.
medallion_architecture.gold: Business-facing aggregates, reporting models. All DQ gates passed, certified datasets only.
medallion_architecture.ownership: NewUM Data team delivers raw data to Bronze. Onco Data team drives Bronze→Silver→Gold.
medallion_architecture.quarantine: Rows failing DQ at Silver route to silver.quarantine.<entity_name> with failure metadata.

unity_catalog_naming.status: Final naming TBD during Unity Catalog governance setup

ingestion_sources.name: NewUM Operational DB
ingestion_sources.entities: Cases, Providers, Eligibility, Case Queuing, Configuration

ingestion_sources.name: DrugsMS (Matis API)
ingestion_sources.entities: MDL, Regimen Library, HCPCS, RxCUI, NDC, GPI, EADL

ingestion_sources.name: Admin Portal: NP Drug Rules
ingestion_sources.entities: NP Drug, Preferred Alternatives, Rule Type, LOB, Dx Group

integration_mechanism.chosen: Batch ingestion via REST API exposed by Data Team
integration_mechanism.description: Data Team exposes REST ingestion API. NewUM calls HTTP POST to push records into Databricks Bronze. NewUM controls push timing. Data Team owns validation, dedup, landing into Delta tables.
integration_mechanism.rationale: Minimal integration surface for NewUM, centralized ingestion on Data Team, API contract serves as versioned boundary.

cases_domain.ownership: NewUM application layer exclusively owns the Case schema in PostgreSQL
cases_domain.scope: Out of Scope in System Design Doc (ownership TBD separately)
cases_domain.integration: Operational case data pushed to Databricks Bronze on daily batch cadence via Data Team REST API. NewUM never reads from Databricks for case processing.

schema_gaps.open_question: Which of the 30 HIGH-priority gaps are resolved before V1.0?

components.mechanism: Azure Storage GRS/RA-GRS; Delta re-register in DR workspace
ADLS GRS + pipeline re-run from Bronze; Time Travel for clean checkpoint
AKS cluster in DR region; Helm chart; DAG code in Git; Postgres geo-redundant backup
DAB in Git; redeploy to DR workspace
Databricks-managed storage; DR workspace linked to ADLS GRS secondary

disaster_recovery.testing: DR runbook tested quarterly (tabletop). Live failover to DR workspace annually.

newum_v1_product.$comment: Product knowledge extracted from NewUM1 Confluence space (50 pages, 422K chars). Captured 2026-03-25.

scope.description: V1.0 establishes foundational platform for fully delegated chemotherapy UM services across Commercial and Medicare LOBs. Functional parity with existing OneUM platforms (MATIS + SmartUM) plus improvements in UX, implementation efficiency, and clinical workflow.
scope.not_live_launch: V1.0 is NOT the final version for live health plan launch. It delivers enough scope to stand up a health plan in production-like environment for extensive user testing.
scope.first_client_migration: EOY 2026 — Geisinger (selected as model payer, Decision #12.0)

case_model.workflow: Case Intake → Case Entry → Case Assignment → Case Processing → Case Finalization → Outbound/Outputs

drug_libraries.mdl: Master Drug Library — HCPCS, RxCUI (IN/PIN/Brand), NDC, GPI, EADL flag, drug type (Main/Ancillary/Both), benefit type
drug_libraries.pdl: Payer Drug Library — per-payer configs: PA Required, PA Exempt, OOS, Per Formulary, Non-Preferred flags, billing method (Medical vs Pharmacy)
drug_libraries.regimen_library: Pre-built regimens with drugs, cycles, dosing defaults; filtered by Diagnosis Group
drug_libraries.eadl: Equivalent Alternatives Drug List — exclusions from alternative switching within same RxCUI family
drug_libraries.drug_id_chain: RxCUI (IN/PIN) → HCPCS → NDC → GPI → Representative NDC

provider_model.search_scope: Provider search crosses all Health Plans (not restricted to case's plan)
provider_model.pos: Place of Service code is payer-configurable

tat_chronos.integration: Service Bus events; NewUM publishes case events, Chronos sends TAT results to Service Bus queue
tat_chronos.parameters: 50+ configurable fields per timer: Payer, LOB, Delegation Level, Request Type, Billing Method, Priority (STAT/Standard), etc.
tat_chronos.abstraction: Jack Hall suggested building abstraction module between newUM and Chronos
tat_chronos.neal_focus: Neal Ruggles focusing on Chronos for 6 weeks

reporting.audit_requirements: Generation timestamp, data source version, logic version, reproducible sampling (seed-based)

auth_delivery.med_vs_pharmacy: Separate auth flows for Med (HCPCS) vs Pharmacy (NDC)

eligibility_etl.pipeline: Payer files (SFTP/FTPS/API) → ETL → EligibilityMS (MS-SQL) → Synapse (lvl0/1/2) → Databricks

eligibility_etl.synapse_medallion: Bronze (lvl0, raw ETL) → Silver (lvl1, mapped/cleaned) → Gold (lvl2, business-ready)

nfg_policies.$comment: Policies from Newfire LLC (staffing partner), NOT from OncoHealth. These apply to all NFG contractors.

documents.title: Asset Management Policy
sections: Mobile Device Policy (MDM required, remote wipe, encryption)
Remote Working (VPN, dedicated workspace, encrypted storage, clean desk)
key_rules: All assets tracked in IT asset inventory with owner assignment
No personal use of company assets without authorization
BYOD requires MDM enrollment, full-disk encryption, strong passcode
Remote workers must use VPN for all company resource access
Removable media (USB etc) prohibited without written approval
Assets returned within 5 business days of termination
Data classified as Confidential, Internal, or Public

documents.title: Information Security Policy / Acceptable Use Policy
sections: Acceptable Use Policy (internet, email, software, social media)
Clear Desk and Clear Screen Policy
key_rules: CEO has ultimate accountability for information security
All employees must report security incidents within 24 hours
No personal software installation on company devices
Social media must not reference client names or proprietary info
Clear desk: sensitive docs locked away when unattended
Clear screen: lock workstation when stepping away (Win+L)
Remote work requires dedicated workspace, no public WiFi without VPN
Violations may result in disciplinary action up to termination

nfg_policies.note: These are NFG-level policies. The 4 OncoHealth-specific policy docs listed in onboarding_documents.policies are still TBD.

workshops_week_2026_03_16.$comment: Knowledge extracted from Teams meeting chats for the week of March 16-20, 2026. NOTE: actual meeting transcripts/recordings were NOT accessible (scraper got DOM dumps only). Content comes from chat messages posted by participants including summaries, action items, and shared analysis.
workshops_week_2026_03_16.transcript_access: BLOCKED — 'You don't have access' or 'No transcript available' for all 10 meetings. Scraper needs transcript/recording permissions.

shared_files: Eligibility.jpg (ER diagram draft from Onco guys)
decisions: Versioning: Hybrid SCD Type 2 — VersionCreatedAt + VersionEndAt replaces EtlStartAt/EtlEndAt. Two time dimensions: (a) version validity window, (b) coverage dates.
Soft delete: DeletedAt ≠ VersionEndAt. VersionEndAt = superseded by newer version. DeletedAt = retroactively voided by payer. Both fields can be set on same record.
API model: Custom model sanity-checked against FHIR (not pure FHIR). Provider FHIR analysis done; Eligibility FHIR mapping not started.
Column naming: Nick compiling change list. 'ODS member key' to be deleted immediately. Clean product-oriented naming, no legacy MATIS names.
open_questions: Manual eligibility records — where do they live? Nick: separate override table (ETL will overwrite, breaks insert-only, audit issues). Jack: same table with source flag. DEFERRED.

Business Grouping ↔ ETL mapping — THE BIGGEST UNRESOLVED QUESTION. How does ETL map variable client data into admin hierarchy? Each payer has different levels/fields/grouping. Nick's Silver uses crosswalk per client. Jack: 'bronze layer problem' — seeding is config, linking is hard.
Person ID grain — What business keys for dedup? Nick: payer-scoped (member ID + payer). Jack: system GUIDs. Nick: GUIDs change every file load without business key grain definition. DEFERRED.
Eligibility ↔ case re-evaluation trigger — when new eligibility arrives mid-case, how to trigger rule set reload? Needs design.
FHIR alignment for Eligibility API — gap analysis not done (Provider is complete). Backlog item.

action_items.action: Write up member ID / person code / coverage ID concepts in Confluence
Share data dictionary PDF with Onco team
Schedule business grouping ↔ ETL mapping prototype session
Update Miro diagram with VersionEndAt, remove EtlStartAt/EtlEndAt
Add business grouping mapping risk to RAID log
Create test data sets for business grouping prototype
Next Eligibility meeting: focus on Person table deep dive + manual override pattern

eligibility_workshop_monday.technical_detail: Nick's BenefitPlan junk dimension proposal for consolidating variable client attributes (same pattern as Address). Jack pushed back — static junk dimension can't guarantee mapping to configuration; business grouping tables exist for dynamic linkage.

decisions: Type 1 vs Type 2 NPI: Proposed separate tables for individual physicians (T1) vs facilities (T2). Nick concerned about duplicating downstream structures (networks, taxonomy). NO FINAL DECISION.
Versioning: Drop separate history tables → single table with version_start_date / version_end_date (consistent with eligibility pattern).

NPI matching V1: NPI-only matching. No probabilistic/candidate matching — too risky since auth files must return provider data exactly as received. Match score/method fields REMOVED from D1/D2 link tables.
Address: Shared address junk dimension + bridge table (consistent with eligibility pattern).
Network tables: T1 and T2 should each have INDEPENDENT network bridge tables (current combined table is wrong).

action_items.action: Confirm versioning pattern with Michal — single table, no separate history
Confirm with BA whether updated_at tracking needed for provider tables
Follow up with app/business team on taxonomy codes needed in UM; remove if not
Independent gap analysis of provider diagram — columns, missing tables, corrections
Update provider diagram: remove history tables, remove match fields, fix network tables, add address bridge, revisit T1/T2 split
Finalize payer-side requirements (site of care, preferred facility rules)
Schedule payer taxonomy workshop + member/person/address meeting
After eligibility finalized, return to finalize provider schema

eligibility_person_member_deep_analysis.$comment: Comprehensive rules & payer taxonomy analysis posted in the meeting chat — appears to be pre-meeting research/synthesis from Confluence V1 PRDs. 30KB of structured content covering all 12 rule domains.
sections_covered: 1. Grain Hierarchy & Inheritance (configurable ranking, lowest wins, effective dating, tie-breaker)
2. Configurations vs Rules (simple params vs IF-THEN logic, Tier 1/2/3 execution)
3. Member Identification & Eligibility Rules (search, coverage dates, RBAC)
4. Scope Validation Rules (6-rule sequential chain, out-of-scope for R1 but fully specified)
5. Payer Drug Configuration Rules (PDL lookup: InsProvider + MajorLOB, benefit type, crossover drugs)
6. Non-Preferred Drug Rules (deepest taxonomy touch: MajorLOB + LOB + ProductLine, mandatory vs voluntary)
7. Auto-Determination Rules (regimen auto-approve, ancillary, case-level, custom regimens always manual)
8. Case Routing & Queue Assignment Rules (30-min degradation strategy)
9. Post-Denial Pathway Rules (classification, routing, timeliness enforcement, integrity)
11. TAT Configuration Rules (Payer + LOB + Delegation + RequestType + BillingMethod)
eligibility_person_member_deep_analysis.dominant_patterns: PDL and scope rules key on InsuranceProvider + MajorLOB. NP rules go deeper into LOB + ProductLine. Configurable grain hierarchy (lowest wins) maps to traversing business hierarchy from leaf (Product) upward to MajorLOB.

critical_design_decisions_pending.status: RESOLVED — ADO #186301 CLOSED March 25, 2026 by Roman Buchyn. CQRS approach implemented.
IN PROGRESS — March 30 meeting evaluated 3 approaches: (1) Lakebase Synced Tables, (2) ADLS+Airflow+PostgreSQL COPY, (3) Apache Iceberg→PostgreSQL. Marc Gale requested cost/effort/speed comparison.

items.question: What was decided for Person ID grain (ADO #185717)? Payer-scoped composite key or system GUID?

items.question: What is the CQRS approach for Eligibility API (ADO #186301)? What replaces with-related-data endpoints?
items.answer: ADO #186301 CLOSED March 25, 2026 by Roman Buchyn. CQRS refactoring completed. Implementation went through Code Review and was merged in sprint 26.03.31.

items.question: How will Dual/DSNP eligibility be represented — split records or separate MLOBs (ADO #183771)?
items.note: Payer taxonomy workshop (2026-03-18) raised this. Inna assigned.

items.question: What happened to Confluence NewUM1 space? Returns 404 since ~2026-03-26.
items.note: Space key NewUM1 returns 404 via REST API. Direct page-by-ID also fails. May be deleted, archived, or permissions revoked. Last good capture: 2026-03-25 (50 pages, 422K chars). Ask Rachel or Inna.

items.question: Which Databricks→PostgreSQL data exchange approach was selected? (Lakebase Synced Tables, ADLS+Airflow+PostgreSQL COPY, or Apache Iceberg→PostgreSQL)
items.note: March 30 meeting 'Exchange data between Databricks and NewUM' (58 min recorded). Michal posted 3 candidates. Marc Gale requested cost/effort/speed comparison. Decision expected April 2026.
